description: Bacterial fatty acid synthesis is a cyclic pathway using the Type II FAS system where each enzyme is a discrete protein, distinct from eukaryotic Type I FAS. The pathway uses acyl carrier protein (ACP) as the central scaffold, acetyl-CoA and malonyl-CoA as building blocks, and proceeds through iterative cycles of condensation, reduction, dehydration, and reduction to elongate fatty acid chains by 2 carbons per cycle. Regulation occurs primarily through feedback inhibition of acetyl-CoA carboxylase by long-chain acyl-ACPs.

name: Fatty Acid Synthesis (Type II FAS)
notes: Represents Type II FAS with 58 nodes. Contains 2 OR gates (initiation vs elongation, chain length check) and demonstrates iterative elongation cycles. ACP serves as central scaffold. Shows feedback inhibition by long-chain acyl-ACPs on acetyl-CoA carboxylase. All Fab enzymes explicitly shown.
scientificAccuracy: This flowchart is based on verified biochemical data from decades of research on bacterial fatty acid synthesis. All enzymatic steps, the role of ACP, and regulatory mechanisms have been validated against primary research sources. The Type II FAS system is one of the best-characterized bacterial metabolic pathways and a major antibiotic target.
